reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures "curves are tangent to each other". A point (x,y) lies on both curves y = f(x) and x = f(y) iff f(x) = y and f(y) = x. At such a point, curve 1 has tangent slope f'(x), and curve 2 (by implicit differentiation) has dy/dx = 1/f'(y), so the tangent lines coincide iff f'(x) · f'(y) = 1. The existential quantifier ∃ x y matches "there is a tangent point" (which is what "tangent to each other" means). The universal quantification over α is implicit via α being a theorem parameter, so "α ∈ solution ↔ P(...)" correctly expresses "find all α". Verified with known answer values (α = 2/3 gives tangent point x=y=1/4 with f'=1; α = 3/2 gives x=y=-1/6 with f'=1). The formulation is logically equivalent to the informal statement.
